requirement: Bootstrap securities-service Go microservice with instrument CRUD and order submission. Follow matching-engine pattern.

tasks.title: Scaffold securities-service Go module
tasks.description:
Create the securities-service Go microservice following the matching-engine pattern.

Create these files:
- src/securities-service/go.mod (module github.com/garudax-platform/securities-service, go 1.25.0)
- src/securities-service/cmd/securities-service/main.go — HTTP server on port 8085, health server on port 9085, register routes, set readyFlag after init. Follow src/matching-engine/cmd/matching-engine/main.go pattern.
- src/securities-service/Dockerfile — multi-stage Go build, same pattern as src/matching-engine/Dockerfile
- src/securities-service/internal/types/types.go — all types from docs/securities-architecture.md section 3 (Instrument Model): Instrument struct with all fields (id, isin, cusip, sedol, ticker, name, asset_class enum, exchange_code, lot_size, tick_size, currency, listing_date, trading_status enum, created_at, updated_at), BondDetails struct, SecurityOrder struct with all fields, TradingStatus enum (ACTIVE/HALTED/SUSPENDED/DELISTED), AssetClass enum (EQUITY/BOND/ETF), OrderType enum (LIMIT/MARKET/STOP/STOP_LIMIT), OrderSide enum (BUY/SELL/SHORT_SELL), OrderStatus enum, TimeInForce enum
- src/securities-service/internal/store/store.go — InstrumentStore interface (List, Get, Create, Update, UpdateStatus), OrderStore interface (Submit, Get, List, Cancel, Amend). InMemoryInstrumentStore and InMemoryOrderStore implementations using sync.RWMutex + map
- src/securities-service/internal/server/server.go — HTTP server setup, route registration, health endpoints (/healthz, /readyz with atomic readyFlag). Follow gateway health.go pattern.

IMPORTANT: Go is at /usr/local/go/bin/go — set PATH. Run: go build ./... to verify compilation.

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T1.md

tasks.title: Instrument CRUD handlers
tasks.description:
Implement the instrument CRUD HTTP handlers in src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers.go (or handlers_instrument.go).

Read docs/securities-openapi.yaml for the exact API contracts. Read .softhouse/handoff/T1.md for the types and store interfaces.

Endpoints:
- GET /api/v1/securities/instruments — list instruments, query params: asset_class, trading_status, exchange_code, search (name/ticker), limit, cursor. Return JSON array with pagination.
- GET /api/v1/securities/instruments/{id} — get by ID. Return 404 if not found.
- POST /api/v1/securities/instruments — create instrument. Validate required fields (ticker, name, asset_class, lot_size, tick_size, currency). Return 201 with created instrument.
- PATCH /api/v1/securities/instruments/{id} — update instrument fields. Return 200.
- PUT /api/v1/securities/instruments/{id}/status — change trading_status. Validate status enum. Return 200.

All handlers should:
- Use the InstrumentStore interface from T1
- Return consistent JSON error responses: {"error": {"code": "NOT_FOUND", "message": "..."}}
- Use snake_case JSON field names (add json tags to all structs)
- Wire routes in server.go

IMPORTANT: Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. Run: go build ./... && go test ./...

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T2.md

tasks.title: Order submission with lot/tick validation
tasks.description:
Implement securities order submission handler in src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_order.go.

Read docs/securities-architecture.md section 4 (Order Types) for validation rules. Read .softhouse/handoff/T1.md and T2.md for context.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/v1/securities/orders — submit order. Validate:
  - instrument_id exists and instrument trading_status is ACTIVE
  - quantity > 0 and quantity % instrument.lot_size == 0 (lot validation)
  - For LIMIT/STOP_LIMIT: price > 0 and price % instrument.tick_size == 0 (tick validation)
  - For MARKET orders: no price required
  - For STOP/STOP_LIMIT: stop_price required and must be valid
  - For SHORT_SELL: reject with 422 if short selling is not enabled (placeholder)
  - side must be BUY, SELL, or SHORT_SELL
  - order_type must be LIMIT, MARKET, STOP, or STOP_LIMIT
  - time_in_force defaults to GTC if not provided
  Return 201 with created order (status=PENDING, filled_quantity=0)

- GET /api/v1/securities/orders — list orders, filter by instrument_id, status, side
- GET /api/v1/securities/orders/{id} — get by ID
- DELETE /api/v1/securities/orders/{id} — cancel order (only if status is PENDING or PARTIALLY_FILLED)

All validation errors return 422 with specific error messages:
- "quantity must be a multiple of lot_size (N)"
- "price must be a multiple of tick_size (N)"
- "instrument is not active for trading"

Wire routes in server.go.

IMPORTANT: Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. Run: go build ./... && go test ./...

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T3.md

tasks.title: Add securities-service to docker-compose.yml
tasks.description:
Add the securities-service to docker-compose.yml following the existing service patterns.

Read docker-compose.yml to understand the existing conventions (health checks, networks, depends_on, env vars).

Add:
- securities-service container: build from src/securities-service/Dockerfile, port 8085 (HTTP), port 9085 (metrics/health), healthcheck using x-healthcheck-http anchor, depends_on postgres, part of garudax-network
- Add appropriate environment variables (DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD matching other services)

Also add a gateway route proxy entry if the gateway uses a config file to route to backends (check how other services are routed through the gateway).

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T4.md

tasks.title: Unit tests for instruments and orders
tasks.description:
Write Go unit tests for the securities-service.

Read .softhouse/handoff/T1.md, T2.md, T3.md for what was implemented.

Create test files:

1. src/securities-service/internal/store/store_test.go:
- Test InMemoryInstrumentStore: Create, Get, List (with filters), Update, UpdateStatus
- Test InMemoryOrderStore: Submit, Get, List (with filters), Cancel
- Test concurrent access (goroutines calling Create/List simultaneously)

2. src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_test.go:
- Test instrument CRUD via HTTP: POST create → GET verify → PATCH update → GET verify
- Test instrument listing with filters (asset_class, trading_status)
- Test 404 for non-existent instrument
- Test validation errors (missing required fields)

3. src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_order_test.go:
- Test order submission success
- Test lot_size validation: quantity=7 with lot_size=5 → 422
- Test tick_size validation: price=10.03 with tick_size=0.05 → 422
- Test order on halted instrument → 422
- Test order cancellation (PENDING → CANCELLED)
- Test cancel non-cancellable order → 409
- Test market order (no price required)
- Test stop-limit order (stop_price required)

Target: 60%+ coverage on business logic packages.

IMPORTANT: Go at /usr/local/go/bin/go. Run: go test ./... -cover

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T5.md
